title: GIF Freak – Client/API Architecture

children.text: GIF Freak models a reactive client that consumes a third-party API through an asynchronous request and render cycle.
UI drives intent.
Search interaction updates client state instead of directly mutating the view.
Network access is isolated.
API communication lives behind an async fetch layer, keeping transport concerns separate from rendering concerns.
Rendering is state-driven.
Once the response returns, the state manager becomes the single source of truth for what the interface renders next.
Stock Memer combines external market data with user-generated content through a layered frontend architecture that separates presentation, state management, and persistence.
External market data feeds application state.
Stock pricing and related inputs arrive from a third-party API and are normalized into the client state layer.
State coordinates multiple UI surfaces.
The charting and meme-generation experiences share a common application model instead of each managing disconnected data pipelines.
Persistence is selective.
Firebase stores user-created artifacts and supports continuity between content creation and later retrieval.

title: Compatibility Engine Architecture

children.text: The compatibility engine is framed as a staged scoring pipeline that transforms raw survey answers into ranked outcomes and explanatory output.
Normalization makes inputs comparable.
Survey responses are standardized before scoring so heterogeneous answers can participate in the same downstream model.
Feature modeling and weighting remain explicit.
The engine surfaces its scoring stages instead of collapsing them into a black-box function, which makes the logic easier to tune and explain.
Output is both ranked and interpretable.
A ranking resolver determines final ordering while a transparency layer provides reasoning context for the result.
Architectural intent.
This hackathon prototype models a voice-driven repair assistant as a closed operational loop rather than a one-shot speech demo.
Input abstraction layer.
Technician speech becomes the primary control surface, removing the need to touch a screen or keyboard in the middle of a repair task.
Speech is normalized into intent.
Audio is transcribed and interpreted through an NLP stage so free-form commands can map to structured workflow actions.
Cloud logic holds workflow state.
Lambda functions act as a lightweight orchestration layer that can evaluate prior step context before issuing the next instruction.
Guidance re-enters the physical workflow.
The system returns spoken instructions so the operator can continue working hands-free and immediately feed the next action back into the same loop.
Even under hackathon constraints, the design hinted at a scalable event-driven workflow assistant for industrial repair and guided maintenance.

sequenceDiagram

participant Tech as Technician
participant Mic as Voice Capture
participant STT as Speech-to-Text
participant NLP as NLP Engine
participant Lambda as AWS Lambda
participant Engine as Instruction Engine
participant Audio as Audio Output

Tech ->> Mic: Speak repair command
Mic ->> STT: Capture audio stream
STT -->> NLP: Transcribed text
NLP ->> Lambda: Structured intent
Lambda ->> Engine: Resolve current repair step
Engine -->> Audio: Next instruction
Audio -->> Tech: Spoken guidance

children.text: Lifecycle view.
This diagram isolates the command path itself: capture, transcription, intent extraction, orchestration, and spoken feedback.
Human input remains natural.
The technician never needs to convert repair intent into rigid command syntax up front.
Interpretation and execution are separated.
Speech-to-text and NLP stages handle understanding, while downstream logic focuses on repair-state resolution and response generation.
Output closes the loop.
Audio guidance is not a terminal event; it directly informs the next technician action and keeps the workflow cyclical.
Purpose.
The editor acts as a browser-native instructional IDE that keeps lesson content, code authoring, runtime execution, and persistence in a single bounded workflow.
Authoring model.
Markdown-based lesson content is rendered alongside an Ace-powered editor so students can read, write, and test code without leaving the learning context.
Execution is routed by project type.
Web projects run in a sandboxed iframe while Python exercises execute through an in-browser runtime, allowing the same workspace to support multiple instructional modes.
Feedback is immediate.
Rendered output and terminal feedback stay adjacent to the editor, reducing context switching and shortening the loop between attempt and correction.
Persistence is explicit.
Saving is treated as a first-class user action and writes project state to S3 so work remains available across classrooms, devices, and grading flows.
The design balances IDE-level capability with classroom constraints by isolating execution paths while preserving a single coherent teaching surface.
Purpose.
The model separates user identity, personal ownership, and institutional entitlement so multi-tenant access rules do not corrupt individual project data.
Identity and ownership.
Users exist independently of organizations, which means they can retain personal projects even when their classroom or school access changes.
Licenses govern capability, not identity.
Organizational licenses determine whether classroom tooling is interactive, but they do not erase underlying user or project records.
Expiration degrades safely.
An expired subscription moves institutional work into read-only mode so historical artifacts remain visible without allowing uncontrolled mutation.
Separating identity, ownership, and licensing created a scalable foundation for supporting multiple schools without entangling user data with subscription state.

User[Authenticated
User] ==> Entry[Classrooms

Entry ==>|Teacher| Teacher

Teacher ==> Dashboard
Student ==> Dashboard
Dashboard ==> Lessons ==> Resolver
Resolver ==>|Existing Work| Existing
Resolver ==>|No Prior Work| Clone ==> Grade

children.text: Purpose.
This flow shows how classroom context resolves into executable student work while preserving deterministic behavior for both teachers and students.
Role-aware entry.
Both user types enter through the same top-level classroom surface, then split into role-specific lists before converging at a shared dashboard model.
Project resolution is deterministic.
When a lesson is selected, the resolver either loads prior work or clones a fresh project from the lesson template using the same decision path every time.
Assessment metadata is created with the project.
New student work is initialized alongside grading context so instructional progress and evaluation remain attached from the start.
The resolver removed friction between instruction and execution by making project startup predictable regardless of user role or prior work state.
Composable curriculum architecture.
The system models curriculum as a strict hierarchy from organization to lesson, which keeps authoring structure stable while allowing classrooms to consume content without owning it.
Structure is centralized.
Organizations manage dashboards, courses, units, and lessons in one compositional chain rather than duplicating content into every classroom.
Execution is isolated.
Classrooms consume lesson outputs and templates while leaving the source curriculum graph intact, protecting active instruction from structural edits.
Templates provide indirection.
Lessons can evolve through stable template references and resource attachments instead of mutating classroom copies directly.
This composition model made curriculum reuse practical across organizations while preserving clear boundaries between authoring and live classroom execution.
This project treats a Raspberry Pi greenhouse controller as a
deterministic closed-loop control system
, not just a collection of GPIO scripts. Sensor input, policy evaluation, and relay state updates are modeled as explicit phases in a recurring cycle.
Sense.
The controller gathers environmental telemetry from the DHT11 sensor on a recurring interval.
Decide.
Readings are interpreted against a growth-mode configuration so thresholds remain data-driven rather than hardcoded into the loop.
Act.
Relay state is mutated only when the desired outcome differs from the current physical state, which reduces unnecessary switching and stabilizes the environment.
The important architectural move is separating environmental policy from hardware control so new growing strategies can be added as configuration instead of rewrites.

sequenceDiagram

participant Sensor as DHT11 Sensor
participant ControlLoop as Control Loop
participant Logic as Threshold Evaluator
participant Relays as GPIO Relay Board
participant Devices as Fan / Vent / Humidity Devices

loop Continuous control cycle
ControlLoop ->> Sensor: Poll temperature + humidity
Sensor -->> ControlLoop: Current readings
ControlLoop ->> Logic: Evaluate thresholds

alt Temperature above target
Logic -->> ControlLoop: Cooling required
ControlLoop ->> Relays: Enable cooling relays
Relays ->> Devices: Increase airflow / cooling
else Temperature below target
Logic -->> ControlLoop: Reduce airflow
ControlLoop ->> Relays: Disable cooling relays
Relays ->> Devices: Lower airflow
else Humidity outside range
Logic -->> ControlLoop: Correct humidity
ControlLoop ->> Relays: Toggle humidity device
Relays ->> Devices: Apply humidity correction
else Stable environment
Logic -->> ControlLoop: Hold state
end
end

children.text: Autonomous environmental control.
The greenhouse automation system functions as a continuous regulation loop that senses, evaluates, and actuates without requiring manual intervention after configuration.
Sensing layer.
Temperature and humidity readings provide the raw environmental signal that drives every control decision.
Decision layer.
Threshold logic compares live telemetry to target bands and determines whether cooling, airflow reduction, humidity correction, or no action is appropriate.
Actuation layer.
GPIO-connected relays convert software state changes into physical device control over fans, vents, and humidity hardware.
The key quality here is not just automation, but predictable automation: every device change is traceable back to the same threshold-evaluation cycle.
This diagram shows the
client-side encryption pipeline
: plaintext input is routed through mode resolution, cipher configuration, deterministic rotation, and metadata tagging before output is produced.
All transformation occurs locally in the browser, so the original plaintext never needs to leave the device.
Mode resolution:
The UI determines that the encryption path should execute and gathers the configuration required for that operation.
Cipher configuration:
A versioned alphabet and magic number provide the inputs needed to make rotation deterministic and reversible.
Self-describing output:
The final tag carries enough metadata to support automatic decryption later without storing external keys or server-side session state.
The mobile view focuses on the
decryption path
: detect the tag, extract metadata, resolve the correct alphabet version, and reverse the rotation per character.

sequenceDiagram

participant User
participant UI
participant Parser
participant Resolver
participant Engine
participant Output

User ->> UI: Paste encrypted text
UI ->> Parser: Detect encryption tag
Parser ->> Resolver: Extract version + magic number
Resolver ->> Engine: Resolve alphabet set
Engine ->> Engine: Reverse character rotation
Engine ->> Output: Produce decrypted text
Output -->> User: Render result

children.text: This sequence diagram shows how Enigma
automatically detects and reverses encrypted input
using the metadata embedded in the output tag.
Decryption is intentionally frictionless: users do not need to remember which alphabet version or magic number was used during encryption.

title: Narrative Domain Architecture

children.text: The campaign system is modeled as a hierarchical domain with clearly bounded aggregates. Each level owns its structural scope while referencing the next narrative layer below it.
Storybook is the aggregate root.
It defines the top-level campaign boundary and coordinates the consistency of acts, rooms, and encounters beneath it.
Narrative layers scale downward cleanly.
Acts contain rooms, rooms contain encounters, and encounters attach tactical context like opponents and treasure without flattening the full story into one structure.
This domain shape keeps branching narrative content extensible while still giving the data model a clear root and ownership chain.
